title: Resolution engine — wire solver into eval pipeline
analysis:
The Engine in reify-eval/src/lib.rs currently has eval(), check(), and build() methods. eval() does two-pass evaluation: Pass 1 evaluates Param defaults and Auto cells (Auto cells become (Undef, Auto)), Pass 2 evaluates Let bindings. check() calls eval() then runs constraints through ConstraintChecker. build() calls check() then does geometry.

The ConstraintSolver trait, ResolutionProblem, AutoParam, and SolveResult types are already defined in reify-types/src/constraint.rs and re-exported from reify_types. The DimensionalSolver in reify-constraints implements ConstraintSolver. ResolutionNodeData, NodeId::Resolution, CachedResult::Resolution variants, and demand/dep support for Resolution nodes all exist from task 20. The EvaluationGraph has a resolutions PersistentMap but from_templates() does not populate it.

Approach: (1) Add Option<Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>> to Engine via with_solver() builder. (2) Add resolution phase at end of eval() after two-pass evaluation. Resolution scans template value_cells for Auto kind, finds constraints whose dependency traces reference auto params, builds ResolutionProblem, calls solver. On Solved: updates values map and snapshot values to (resolved_val, Determined), creates child snapshot with SnapshotProvenance::Resolution provenance, re-evaluates let bindings. (3) check() naturally sees resolved values since it calls eval(). (4) Handle Infeasible/NoProgress with diagnostics. (5) Add resolved_params tracking to EvalResult/CheckResult/BuildResult.

ResolutionNodes in the graph are NOT populated by from_templates in this task — the resolution phase scans value cells and constraints directly. Incremental resolution support can be added later.

Key files: lib.rs (Engine, eval, check, build, result types), snapshot.rs (Snapshot), deps.rs (extract_dependency_trace), cache.rs (CachedResult::Resolution), mocks.rs (MockConstraintSolver).

--- REVIEW FIX ANALYSIS ---
Review found two blocking issues (same root cause): In the Solved branch of the resolution phase (lib.rs lines 320-385), all record_evaluation() calls use VersionId(version_id) — the pre-resolution version allocated at eval() start (line 156). But the snapshot's version is updated to VersionId(res_version_id) — a freshly allocated post-resolution version (line 354). This creates a mismatch: snapshot.version = N+1 but all cache entries have basis_version = N. The try_fast_path() in CacheStore checks basis_version == current_version (strict equality), so on any subsequent edit_param() call (which allocates version N+2), the fast-path check for resolution-phase entries becomes N != N+2, forcing ALL let bindings through the full dependency-trace path regardless of whether they depend on the changed param. This completely negates incremental evaluation for post-resolution modules.

Fix: Move res_version_id allocation BEFORE the auto-param and let-binding cache recording loops, and use VersionId(res_version_id) in ALL record_evaluation() calls within the Solved branch. This ensures cache entries are stamped with the same version as the snapshot, allowing the fast-path to succeed on subsequent edit_param() calls when the edited param doesn't affect those cached nodes.

prerequisites.description: Add MockConstraintSolver to reify-test-support/src/mocks.rs. Three constructors: new_solved(HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>) for Solved, new_infeasible(Vec<Diagnostic>) for Infeasible, new_no_progress(String) for NoProgress. Implements ConstraintSolver trait by returning cloned predetermined result. Include unit tests for each constructor. Follows existing MockConstraintChecker pattern.

Add resolved_params: std::collections::HashMap<ValueCellId, reify_types::Value> field to EvalResult, CheckResult, and BuildResult in reify-eval/src/lib.rs. Initialize to HashMap::new() in all existing construction sites: eval() line 266, edit_param() line 391, eval_cached() line 588, check() line 631, build() line 703. Non-behavioral change — all existing tests pass with empty resolved_params.

steps.description: Write failing test `engine_with_solver_accepts_solver` in new file crates/reify-eval/tests/resolution.rs: Create Engine::new(MockConstraintChecker, None).with_solver(MockConstraintSolver::new_solved(...)). Build module with auto_param('S','x',Type::length()) via TopologyTemplateBuilder. Call eval(). Assert no panic, x is in values (may still be Undef — solver integration comes later).

Add `solver: Option<Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>>` field to Engine struct. Add `pub fn with_solver(mut self, solver: Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>) -> Self` that sets the field and returns self. Update Engine::new() to init solver: None. Add import for ConstraintSolver from reify_types.

Write failing test `resolve_single_auto_param`: Build module with auto_param('S','thickness',Type::length()), constraint 'thickness > 2mm' (gt(value_ref('S','thickness'), literal(mm(2.0)))), constraint 'thickness < 20mm' (lt(value_ref('S','thickness'), literal(mm(20.0)))). Create Engine with MockConstraintSolver::new_solved({thickness: mm(5.0)}). Call eval(). Assert thickness value == mm(5.0) (not Undef).

Add resolution phase to eval() after pass 2 (let bindings). For each template: (1) collect auto_ids = value_cells where kind==Auto as HashSet<ValueCellId>, (2) skip if empty or solver is None, (3) find constraints whose extract_dependency_trace reads intersect auto_ids, (4) build Vec<AutoParam> from graph nodes (id, cell_type, bounds=None), (5) build ResolutionProblem { auto_params, constraints: filtered_pairs, current_values: values.clone(), objective: None }, (6) call solver.solve(&problem), (7) on Solved: insert resolved values into values map. Import AutoParam, ResolutionProblem, SolveResult, ConstraintSolver from reify_types.

Write failing test `resolved_param_determinacy_and_provenance`: Same module setup as step-3. After eval(), get engine.snapshot(). Assert snapshot.values has thickness → (mm(5.0), DeterminacyState::Determined). Assert snapshot.provenance matches SnapshotProvenance::Resolution { scope: 'S', resolved: {thickness_id}, parent: SnapshotId(0) }.

In resolution phase Solved branch: (1) update snapshot.values with (resolved_val, Determined) for each resolved param, (2) allocate new snapshot_id and version_id, (3) set snapshot.provenance = SnapshotProvenance::Resolution { scope: template.name.clone(), resolved: resolved_ids_set, parent: parent_snapshot_id }, (4) record CachedResult::Value(val, Determined) in cache for each resolved param node.

Write failing test `let_binding_re_evaluated_after_resolution`: Build module with auto_param('S','x',Type::length()), let_binding('S','y',Type::length(), BinOp::Mul(value_ref('S','x'), literal(Value::Real(2.0)))), constraint 'x > 2mm'. MockConstraintSolver returns x=mm(10.0). After eval(), assert y value ≈ 0.02 (10mm * 2 in SI). This fails until let re-evaluation is added.

After Solved branch updates values map, re-run pass 2 (let binding evaluation): iterate template.value_cells for Let kind with default_expr, re-evaluate expr against updated values, update snapshot.values and cache entries. This is idempotent for lets not depending on resolved params. Update cache with new dependency traces and CachedResult.

Write failing test `check_reports_satisfied_after_resolution`: Build module with auto_param('S','thickness',Type::length()), constraint gt(value_ref, literal(mm(2.0))). Use SimpleConstraintChecker (real checker) and MockConstraintSolver returning thickness=mm(5.0). Call engine.check(). Assert constraint_results[0].satisfaction == Satisfied. Verifies check() integration with resolution.

Verify check() integration works. check() calls eval() which now resolves, then passes resolved values to constraint_checker.check(). SimpleConstraintChecker evaluates 0.005 > 0.002 → true → Satisfied. Propagate resolved_params from eval_result into CheckResult.resolved_params. May need no changes beyond resolved_params propagation.

Write failing test `resolve_multiple_auto_params`: Build module with auto_param('S','a',Type::length()), auto_param('S','b',Type::length()), constraint gt(value_ref('S','a'), literal(mm(1.0))), constraint gt(value_ref('S','b'), literal(mm(2.0))). MockConstraintSolver returns {a:mm(5.0), b:mm(10.0)}. After eval(), assert a==mm(5.0), b==mm(10.0). Assert resolved_params has 2 entries.

Verify multi-param resolution works with existing implementation. Step-4 impl collects ALL auto params per template into a single ResolutionProblem. MockConstraintSolver returns all values. Should work without changes. Fix any issues found.

Write failing test `solver_infeasible_produces_diagnostics`: Build module with auto_param and constraint. MockConstraintSolver::new_infeasible(vec![Diagnostic::error('constraints are infeasible')]). After eval(), assert thickness is Undef. Assert diagnostics contains message 'constraints are infeasible'. Assert resolved_params is empty.

In resolution phase, handle SolveResult::Infeasible { diagnostics: solver_diags }: extend eval diagnostics with solver_diags. Auto params remain (Undef, Auto) in snapshot. resolved_params stays empty. Snapshot provenance stays Initial (no Resolution provenance on failure).

Write failing test `solver_no_progress_produces_warning`: Build module with auto_param and constraint. MockConstraintSolver::new_no_progress('iteration limit reached'). After eval(), assert auto param is Undef. Assert diagnostics contains a warning with text containing 'iteration limit reached'. Assert resolved_params is empty.

In resolution phase, handle SolveResult::NoProgress { reason }: push Diagnostic::warning(format!('Constraint solver made no progress: {}', reason)) to eval diagnostics. Auto params remain (Undef, Auto). resolved_params stays empty.

Write test `no_solver_backward_compatible`: Build module with auto_param('S','x',Type::length()) and constraint. Create Engine WITHOUT with_solver() (solver is None). Call eval(). Assert x is Undef with Auto determinacy in snapshot. Assert diagnostics is empty. Assert resolved_params is empty. Confirms backward compatibility.

Verify no-solver case works. Resolution phase is guarded by solver.is_some() check, so it's skipped when solver is None. Auto params remain (Undef, Auto). No changes expected — this step confirms existing behavior.

Write test `eval_result_tracks_resolved_params`: Same setup as step-3 (single auto param resolved). Assert eval_result.resolved_params == {thickness_id: mm(5.0)}. Call check() and assert check_result.resolved_params matches. Call build() with MockGeometryKernel and assert build_result.resolved_params matches. Verifies resolved_params propagation through all result types.

In eval() Solved branch: set resolved_params = solver_values.clone() (the HashMap from SolveResult::Solved). Return EvalResult { values, diagnostics, resolved_params }. In check(): copy eval_result.resolved_params to CheckResult. In build(): copy check_result.resolved_params to BuildResult. Ensure edit_param() and eval_cached() return empty resolved_params.

Write failing test `resolution_cache_version_matches_snapshot` in crates/reify-eval/tests/resolution.rs: Build module with auto_param('S','x',Type::length()), let_binding('S','y',Type::length(), x*2), constraint gt(x, mm(2.0)). MockConstraintSolver returns x=mm(5.0). Call eval(). Then inspect: (1) get snapshot version via engine.snapshot().unwrap().version, (2) get cache entry for auto param x via engine.cache_store().get(&NodeId::Value(x_id)), (3) get cache entry for let binding y via engine.cache_store().get(&NodeId::Value(y_id)). Assert both cache entries' basis_version == snapshot.version. This test exposes the bug where resolution-phase cache entries are stamped with the pre-resolution version_id while the snapshot uses res_version_id, creating a phantom version gap that breaks the fast-path invariant.

Fix cache version consistency in the Solved branch of eval()'s resolution phase (lib.rs lines 320-385). The bug: res_snapshot_id/res_version_id are allocated at lines 349-352 (AFTER the auto-param cache recording loop at lines 340-345), and the let-binding re-eval loop at lines 378-383 also uses VersionId(version_id). Fix: (1) Move the res_snapshot_id/res_version_id allocation to immediately after 'let mut resolved_ids = ...' (BEFORE the auto-param loop), (2) set snapshot.id/version/provenance immediately after allocation, (3) change the record_evaluation call at line 343 (auto-param loop) to use VersionId(res_version_id), (4) change the record_evaluation call at line 381 (let-binding re-eval loop) to use VersionId(res_version_id). This ensures all cache entries from the resolution phase have basis_version == snapshot.version == VersionId(res_version_id), restoring the invariant that try_fast_path relies on.

Write test `incremental_fast_path_works_after_resolution` in crates/reify-eval/tests/resolution.rs: Build module with auto_param('S','x',Type::length()), param('S','z',Type::length(), default=mm(1.0)), let_binding('S','y',Type::length(), x*2), let_binding('S','w',Type::length(), z*3), constraint gt(x, mm(2.0)). MockConstraintSolver returns x=mm(5.0). Call eval(). Then call edit_param(z_id, mm(2.0)). Assert w value == mm(2.0)*3 = 0.006 SI (updated). Assert y value == mm(5.0)*2 = 0.01 SI (unchanged — still uses resolved x). Also verify that the cache entry for y has basis_version == snapshot.version from the resolution phase (not the edit_param version), confirming the fast-path was usable. This test validates the practical impact: incremental evaluation correctly skips unaffected let bindings after resolution.

Verify that step-23's test passes with the fix from step-22. The edit_param() fast-path check for y's cache entry now succeeds: y's basis_version (res_version_id from eval) won't match edit_param's new version_id, BUT y is not in the dirty cone (y depends on x, not z), so it's not in the eval_set and its cached value is simply read from the snapshot. The key correctness property is that w (which depends on z) IS re-evaluated while y (which depends only on resolved x) is NOT. No additional code changes expected — this step confirms the fix from step-22 restores correct incremental behavior.

design_decisions.decision: Embed resolution phase in eval(), not check() or build()
design_decisions.rationale: eval() returns fully-resolved values so check() and build() naturally see resolved values. This maintains the existing layering: eval() → check() → build(). Constraints are checked against already-resolved values in check(), producing a single constraint check that reports the final state (Satisfied). The task pseudocode shows resolution before geometry, which this achieves.

design_decisions.decision: with_solver() builder method rather than new constructor parameter
design_decisions.rationale: Backward compatible — existing Engine::new(checker, kernel) callers need no changes. Solver is optional (most tests don't need it). Builder pattern (self-consuming method returning Self) is idiomatic Rust for optional configuration. Matches how geometry_kernel is already optional.

design_decisions.decision: Resolution phase scans graph directly instead of populating ResolutionNodes from from_templates
design_decisions.rationale: from_templates() doesn't currently populate ResolutionNodes and adding that introduces complexity not needed for this task. The resolution phase can scan value_cells for Auto kind and constraint dependency traces for references to auto params. The ResolutionNode infrastructure from task 20 is available for future incremental resolution (edit_param triggering re-resolution). Keeps this task focused on core wiring.

design_decisions.decision: Re-run entire let binding loop after resolution rather than selective re-evaluation
design_decisions.rationale: For cold-start eval(), re-running all let bindings is simple, correct, and the performance cost is negligible (only evaluated in initial eval, not incremental). Using dirty/demand infrastructure for selective re-evaluation is designed for incremental (edit_param) scenarios. The re-run is idempotent for let bindings not depending on resolved params.

design_decisions.decision: Only include constraints that directly reference auto params in ResolutionProblem
design_decisions.rationale: Constraints referencing let bindings that transitively depend on auto params are excluded because the DimensionalSolver substitutes auto param values in current_values but doesn't propagate through let binding expressions. Direct constraints cover the v0.1 use case ('param thickness = auto, constraint thickness > 2mm'). Transitive resolution can be added in M5 when the language supports more complex dependency patterns.

Auto params and constraints are per-template in the compiler output. ResolutionProblem is built per-template, and SnapshotProvenance::Resolution carries the template name as scope. Multiple templates with auto params produce separate solve calls, which is correct since their auto params are independent. The scope field in provenance enables debugging and audit.

design_decisions.decision: Resolution creates child snapshot with new ID/version (not in-place mutation)
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the existing edit_param() pattern: clone snapshot (O(1) via PersistentMap structural sharing), update values and provenance. Preserves the provenance chain for undo/redo and audit trails. The parent snapshot ID links back to the Initial snapshot. If multiple templates resolve, each produces a new child snapshot.

design_decisions.decision: Solver failure (Infeasible/NoProgress) is non-fatal — diagnostics emitted, eval continues
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly requires 'Solver failure produces diagnostics, not panics'. Auto params remain (Undef, Auto) on failure. Constraints referencing unresolved autos evaluate to Indeterminate (existing behavior via SimpleConstraintChecker Undef→Indeterminate mapping). Geometry realization can still proceed with Undef values, producing degraded output rather than crashing.

design_decisions.decision: Allocate res_version_id BEFORE recording resolution-phase cache entries, not after
design_decisions.rationale: Review found that the original implementation allocated res_version_id after recording auto-param cache entries with the pre-resolution version_id, and the let-binding re-eval loop also used the pre-resolution version_id. This created a version mismatch: snapshot.version = res_version_id but all cache entries had basis_version = version_id. The try_fast_path check (basis_version == current_version) would never match for resolution-phase entries, forcing all let bindings through the full dependency-trace path on every subsequent edit_param(), completely negating incremental evaluation. Fix: allocate res_version_id first, use it in all record_evaluation calls within the Solved branch.

reuse.how: Clone current snapshot (O(1)), assign new snapshot_id/version_id, set SnapshotProvenance::Resolution. Same structural sharing via PersistentMap. Follow the exact pattern: clone → update values → update provenance → store.
Use to determine which constraints reference auto params. Call extract_dependency_trace(&constraint.expr) and check if any reads are in the auto_param_ids HashSet.
Follow same pattern for MockConstraintSolver: predetermined result, builder constructors (new_solved, new_infeasible, new_no_progress), implement ConstraintSolver trait returning cloned result.
Already exists. Build test modules with auto params. Combine with .constraint() and expression builders (gt, lt, and, value_ref, literal) for test module construction.

reuse.what: Expression builders (gt, lt, and, value_ref, literal, binop)
reuse.how: Build constraint expressions in tests: gt(value_ref('S','x'), literal(mm(2.0))). Also BinOp::Mul via CompiledExpr::binop() for let binding expressions referencing auto params.
mm(5.0) creates Value::Scalar with LENGTH dimension and SI value 0.005. Use for solver mock return values and expected value assertions.
Already defined with { scope: String, resolved: HashSet<ValueCellId>, parent: SnapshotId }. Use directly when creating resolution snapshot provenance.
Already defined as Resolution(HashMap<ValueCellId, Value>). Use for recording resolution results in the cache store.
Use Diagnostic::error() for infeasible diagnostics, Diagnostic::warning() for no-progress warnings. Both take impl Into<String>.

Use as real constraint checker in integration test (step-9) to verify constraints evaluate to Satisfied after resolution. Evaluates constraint expressions against current values and maps bool results to Satisfaction.
Use engine.cache_store().get(&NodeId::Value(id)) to inspect NodeCache entries in tests. NodeCache.basis_version can be compared against snapshot.version to verify the cache version invariant.
